_note: Citation-level references for the intellectual-history view. Author/year/title/venue/DOI and annotations are drawn from Micah's own merged annotated bibliography (Google Drive: 'annotated_bibliography_merged_tagged' and the AECT annotated-bib doc). 'tradition' links each source to a lineage node in traditions.json. 'frictions' uses Micah's own Noetic/Rhetorical/Existential tags. verified=true means the citation came from Micah's compiled bibliography; verify page numbers against originals before quoting in the proposal.

references.author: Ong, W. J.
references.title: Orality and literacy: The technologizing of the word
references.annotation: Shifts in consciousness across orality and literacy; the spine extended in this dissertation to algorithmic secondary orality and tertiary algorithmicity, and to pedagogical friction.

references.author: Innis, H. A.
references.title: The bias of communication
references.annotation: Time- versus space-biased media; motivates reintroducing slowness and durability against frictionless circulation.

references.author: McLuhan, M.
references.title: Understanding media: The extensions of man
references.annotation: The medium is the message; the tetrad offers a lens for AI's effects in the classroom.

references.author: Kittler, F. A.
references.title: Gramophone, film, typewriter
references.annotation: Materialist thesis privileging circuits and code over content; cognition shaped by media infrastructure.

references.author: Ernst, W.
references.title: Digital memory and the archive
references.annotation: Media-archaeological account of machine time; frames AI as an epistemic regime, not only a tool.

references.author: Huhtamo, E.; Parikka, J. (Eds.)
references.title: Media archaeology: Approaches, applications, and implications
references.annotation: Historically attuned, materially grounded methods for situating AI within deep time.

references.author: Zielinski, S.
references.title: Deep time of the media: Toward an archaeology of hearing and seeing by technical means
references.annotation: Variantology and non-linear media histories; situates AI within long genealogies rather than rupture narratives.

references.author: Hayles, N. K.
references.title: How we think: Digital media and contemporary technogenesis
references.annotation: Technogenesis; shifting reading practices and the rise of an algorithmic consciousness.

references.author: Ellul, J.
references.title: The technological society
references.annotation: The totalizing logic of efficiency clarifies why schooling needs friction to preserve judgment and human ends.

references.author: Feenberg, A.
references.title: Questioning technology
references.annotation: Critical theory of technology; design encodes values, so friction can act as a democratic intervention.

references.author: Illich, I.
references.title: Tools for conviviality
references.annotation: Tools that extend autonomy and skill; friction keeps AI augmentative rather than substitutive.

references.author: Postman, N.
references.title: Technopoly: The surrender of culture to technology
references.annotation: Warns against ceding cultural authority to technology; preserves space for deliberation.

references.author: Foucault, M.
references.title: The archaeology of knowledge
references.annotation: Discursive formations and conditions of possibility; how AI reshapes what counts as knowledge and evidence.

references.author: Stiegler, B.
references.title: For a new critique of political economy
references.annotation: Cognitive proletarianization; the case for reclaiming cognitive craft against externalized memory.

references.author: Papert, S.
references.title: Mindstorms: Children, computers, and powerful ideas
references.annotation: Constructionism: durable learning comes from building and debugging, a counterweight to frictionless answer generation.

references.author: Dewey, J.
references.title: Experience and education
references.annotation: Embodied, inquiry-driven learning counters over-mediated instruction.

references.author: Kapur, M.
references.title: Productive failure
references.annotation: Guided struggle before instruction improves conceptual understanding and transfer; empirical anchor for productive friction.

references.author: Karpicke, J. D.; Roediger, H. L., III
references.title: The critical importance of retrieval for learning
references.annotation: Large effects of retrieval practice on delayed recall; the cost of offloading retrieval to AI.

references.author: Pariser, E.
references.title: The filter bubble: What the Internet is hiding from you
references.annotation: Personalization and curation logics; the case to diversify inputs and slow judgment. Anchors algorithmic secondary orality.

references.author: Williamson, B.
references.title: Big data in education: The digital future of learning, policy and practice
references.annotation: Data infrastructures and algorithmic governance; locates friction at the policy level.

references.author: Zuboff, S.
references.title: The age of surveillance capitalism: The fight for a human future at the new frontier of power
references.annotation: Data extraction and behavioral prediction; curricular and policy frictions to preserve autonomy.

references.author: Varoufakis, Y.
references.title: Technofeudalism: What killed capitalism
references.annotation: Platform power and rent extraction; friction protects cognitive sovereignty.

references.author: Örtegren, A.
references.title: Philosophical underpinnings of digital citizenship through a postdigital lens
references.annotation: Recalibrates professional digital competence toward ecological, justice-oriented understandings.

references.author: Meyerhofer-Parra, R.; González-Martínez, J.; Peracaula-Bosch, M.
references.title: Postdigital storytelling: Storytelling (within or across) the digital and transmedia field
references.annotation: Defines postdigital storytelling and links narrative design to critical pedagogy.

references.author: Macleroy, V.; Chung, Y.
references.title: How can digital storytelling open up spaces for activist citizenship where young children create stories of hope and resilience across the world?
references.annotation: Multilingual, justice-oriented filmmaking with 8-year-olds fosters activist citizenship; productive friction in practice.

references.author: Bacalja, A.; Beavis, C.; O'Brien, A.
references.title: Shifting landscapes of digital literacy
references.venue: The Australian Journal of Language and Literacy
references.annotation: Conceptual critique of skills-first approaches; argues for critical, creative, data-aware literacies amid platformization and AI.

references.author: Kellner, D.; Share, J.
references.title: Critical media literacy, democracy, and the reconstruction of education
references.annotation: Power-aware, justice-oriented media literacy; friction aligned with civic debate.

references.author: Sellami, I.; Amin, H.; Ozturk, O.; Zaman, A.; Sever, S. D.; Tok, E.
references.title: Digital, localised and human-centred design makerspaces
references.annotation: Mixed-methods evaluation showing gains; cultural localization and SDG alignment.

references.author: Carr, N. G.
references.title: The shallows: What the Internet is doing to our brains
references.annotation: Digital skimming and multitasking undermine deep reading; risks amplified by frictionless AI.

references.author: Wolf, M.
references.title: Reader, come home: The reading brain in a digital world
references.annotation: Neuroscientific case for deep reading; intentional slowness and structured struggle.

references.author: Haidt, J.
references.title: The anxious generation: How the great rewiring of childhood is causing an epidemic of mental illness
references.annotation: Developmental and social evidence: anxiety, attention fragmentation, diminished resilience.

references.author: Rosen, C.
references.title: The extinction of experience: Being human in a disembodied world
references.annotation: Algorithmically curated experiences displace first-hand reality; the case to restore sensory learning.

references.author: Turkle, S.
references.title: Alone together: Why we expect more from technology and less from each other
references.annotation: Relational artifacts and pseudo-sociality; the case for privileging authentic dialogue.

references.author: Virilio, P.
references.title: Speed and politics: An essay on dromology
references.annotation: Speed reorganizes perception and politics; anchor for the speed-depth paradox.

references.author: Husserl, E.
references.title: The crisis of European sciences and transcendental phenomenology
references.annotation: The crisis of meaning when formalized science detaches from the lifeworld; grounds existential abstraction. Verify edition/translation and pages.

references.author: Selwyn, N.
references.title: Should robots replace teachers? AI and the future of education
references.annotation: Critical edtech analysis; situates friction within curriculum, governance, and professionalism.

references.author: Connolly, C.; O'Brien, E.; O'Ceallaigh, T. J.
references.title: Ensuring knowledge sustainability in a digital era: Empowering digital transformation through digital educational leadership
references.annotation: Structural-realist digital educational leadership model; sustainable, equity-focused ecosystems across system levels. Supports infrastructural friction.

references.author: Greene, J. A.; Crompton, H.
references.title: Synthesizing definitions of digital literacy for the Web 3.0
references.annotation: Systematic review yielding a context-sensitive digital literacy definition for AI and decentralized environments.

references.author: An, Y.; Shadarra, J.
references.title: Generative AI integration in K-12 settings: Teachers' perceptions and levels of integration
references.annotation: SAMR-framed interviews map six GenAI use cases; most use remains at Augmentation. Directly relevant to RQ1 and PD design.

references.author: Hu, W.; Gong, R.; Wu, S.; Li, Y.
references.title: A conversational agent based on a contingent teaching model to support collaborative learning activities
references.annotation: Randomized controlled study of CollaBot shows performance gains and nuanced perceptions of AI scaffolding.

references.author: Wiley, D.
references.title: Asking a more productive question about AI and assessment
references.annotation: Editorial reframing evidence quality versus evaluative effort; redesign over prohibition. Supports the productive-friction assessment argument.

references.author: Evmenova, A. S.; Borup, J.; Shin, J. K.
references.title: Harnessing the power of generative AI to support ALL learners
references.annotation: 43% of teachers reported ChatGPT will help make instruction more accessible for all learners; links GenAI to Universal Design for Learning. Relevant to the productive-versus-exclusionary friction distinction.

references.author: Memon, T. D.; Kwan, P.
references.title: A collaborative model for integrating teacher and GenAI into future education
references.annotation: Proposes a teacher-plus-GenAI collaborative model and reviews three real-world case studies.

references.author: Yusuf, H.; Money, A.; Daylamani-Zad, D.
references.title: Pedagogical AI conversational agents in higher education: A conceptual framework and survey of the state of the art
references.annotation: Thematic template analysis of 92 studies; framework spanning pastoral, instructional, and cognitive purposes.

references.author: Martin, F.; Kim, S.; Bolliger, D. U.; DeLarm, J.; et al.
references.title: Assessment types, strategies, and feedback in online higher education courses in the age of artificial intelligence
references.annotation: Instructional designers rated case-study analysis and e-portfolios most effective; exams least effective. Evidence for friction-preserving assessment design.
